title: Kaworus Motivation Purpose
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu's Motivation/Purpose             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Jun 22, 2014 7:50 pm I'm not sure of Kaworu's motvation or his purpose in the story. I personally think he was malicious and was trying to manipulate Shinji, and his role in the story is to relay that unconditional love and kindness will never be handed to you, but I'm probably way off.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 23, 2014 2:22 pm Given that he found the depth of Humanity's deception to be so surprising that it made him rethink his entire position on everything, I think we can take Kaworu as being truthful about everything he says. I believe he really did love and sympathize with Shinji, as an extended metaphor for his fascination with our entire species, but he had a biological imperative to follow, and SEELE made it sound like it was what we wanted, so he didn't really worry about it until he realized he was tricked.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Tue Jun 24, 2014 8:38 pm I thought that Kaworu was an angel and that angels had the mission statement of wiping out humanity. Were they not actually trying to destroy humanity? Were they simply destined to?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 25, 2014 12:00 am It was probably an inevitable conflict, but each individual angel probably had their own intentions. Perhaps some just wanted to find their mom, either to be with her or to fuse with her and become a new seed in her place. Perhaps some were trying to do the same to Lilith, or just wanted to destroy her for revenge. Some angels seemed more interested in killing the pilots, others in trying to understand them. Iruel was more interested in the Magi, and both Israfel and Sandalphon had battle brought to them before they even really did anything at all. In short, it's complicated and unclear... just like everything else in eva.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Jun 25, 2014 11:28 am Tumbling Down wrote: I thought that Kaworu was an angel and that angels had the mission statement of wiping out humanity. Were they not actually trying to destroy humanity? Were they simply destined to?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 25, 2014 12:20 pm Tumbling Down wrote: I thought that Kaworu was an angel and that angels had the mission statement of wiping out humanity. Were they not actually trying to destroy humanity? Were they simply destined to?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jun 25, 2014 12:24 pm As far as Kaworu's role is concerned to Shinji's character arc, I would agree that he does seem to represent the unobtainable and "angelic" nature of unconditional love and kindness. During the series, Shinji was at a low point and he felt isolated; his classmates had been evacuated, Rei is really creepy now that he realizes she's a clone, and Asuka had run away and then found and then hospitalized. Finally there's Misato, who's relationship with him during this part in the series seems to be the most complex, but it seems that Shinji is scared to face her after everything that had happened with Kaji, and then later the bedroom scene. Then along comes Kaworu, out of the blue, who seems genuinely interested and compassionate toward Shinji. Like a poor sucker, Shinji instantly latched onto his affection, because it was exactly the thing he desperately needed at that moment. However, due to the nature of Angelic beings (including the Lilin species as a whole), they cannot actually coexist together. In a strange way, this seems to be commentary about the divinity associated with unconditional love, and that such a thing can't actually exist among real people (we can only try our best to understand and love each other, but even that only goes so far). The inevitable conflict occurred, and Shinji had to give up this new found kindness that he had only briefly known. This creates a huge conflict within Shinji, which is expanded upon during the later character studies in EoTV, but IIRC, Kaworu is basically the perfect human Shinji wants to be and the the kind of person he expects others to be (which is ultimately an unobtainable fantasy), and he regrets having to kill that innocence in favor for "true" humanity's survival. Shinji then has to come to terms with living with other flawed individuals with their own opinions and perspectives, as opposed to living within his own isolated bubble. The rest is pretty much history. Again, this is from the perspective of Kaworu's role to Shinji's character arc in particular, not necessarily a view on Kaworu's objective role in the series, involving Seele, the Angels, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Thu Jun 26, 2014 6:39 am Kaworu admired the Lilin, but his Angel instincts compelled him to initiate Third Impact to reclaim the Earth for the Angels (the struggle with his instincts was explicitly stated in the earlier drafts of episode 24, although it became more subtle in the final version of the episode). However, his feelings for Shinji helped him decide that the Lilin deserved to live, which is why he chose to die so that Shinji and the others would have a chance to survive. He says this outright in the episode: "Now, erase me from this world. If you don't, you will be the ones who are erased. Only one life form will be chosen to survive the time of destruction and be given a future. And you are not a being who should die. Your people need the future." This is also stated in the official book Evangelion Chronicle Side B, in the Character Overview section: "He hopes that Shinji, his friend, will choose the survival of humanity" (please note this is my translation of the French edition rather than the Japanese one... the book is not available yet in English, although supposedly Udon Entertainment will be releasing an English one eventually). Therefore, Kaworu was not a malicious character. He may have been naive because he couldn't think of a better idea (I'm sure a solution that didn't involve dying would have made Shinji a lot happier!), but he was not evil. Further insight into his character can be obtained by reading a translation of his favorite song, "Ode to Joy", which includes the line "a friend loyal unto death"... that line describes Kaworu perfectly.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:12 pm Kaworu's purpose to the story is very clear: He is introduced as a device to make Shinji break the last of his deeply held principles -- respecting the sanctity of life -- and finally shatter the last of Shinji's sense of self worth. While the character has other aspects, and is built upon more in EoE, his main purpose is to complete the deconstruction of Shinji as a character by shattering the latter's personal morality and self-respect.
